wiki.summary: “iPod Touch“ is a single and the second overall track from I Love My Computer, released with an accompanying music video. Nina grows closer with her computer/iPod and explores music, slowly forming a relationship with her computer. “‘iPod Touch’ is about stumbling upon EDM online when I was twelve and it cracking my whole world open. None of my friends from school liked electronic music so it felt like my secret or something. I started spending a lot of late nights on the computer, trying to soak up everything I could about it. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: “iPod Touch“ is a single and the second overall track from I Love My Computer, released with an accompanying music video. Nina grows closer with her computer/iPod and explores music, slowly forming a relationship with her computer. “‘iPod Touch’ is about stumbling upon EDM online when I was twelve and it cracking my whole world open. None of my friends from school liked electronic music so it felt like my secret or something. I started spending a lot of late nights on the computer, trying to soak up everything I could about it. I really just wanted to swim in it and it was when I started learning how to produce music. I didn’t realise at the time how much a random discovery would end up shaping my life. It is the next single from my forthcoming debut album, which is called I Love My Computer—it comes out in August. This song is about going to high school in Gosford, sneaking out to smoke, getting the bus to the beach after school, going to the shops, having an iPod Touch, shopping at Supré; and it sounds like something I would have loved at that pivotal time which makes me really happy.“ — via Triple J premiere “When I was in high school, I got really into electronic music, but also pretty niche SoundCloud electronic music, and I didn’t have any friends that were into it. It felt like all my favourite songs were my secrets, ‘cause I didn’t have anyone to share them with. In that song, there’s a reference to a Porter Robinson track from 2012 that was my favourite song when I was 12 or however old I was.“ — via Apple Music Read more on Last.fm.
